purpose: Baseline comparison across simple ML models on the same model-ready dataset. These are first-pass sanity checks before deeper tuning.

legend.TN: Actual not readmitted <30 and predicted not readmitted <30
legend.FP: Actual not readmitted <30 but predicted readmitted <30
legend.FN: Actual readmitted <30 but predicted not readmitted <30

diagnostics: Low precision: many predicted readmissions are false positives.
Class imbalance is strong; accuracy can look high while minority recall remains weak.

legend.FP: Actual not readmitted <30 but predicted readmitted <30
legend.FN: Actual readmitted <30 but predicted not readmitted <30

notes: Random Forest uses class_weight=balanced_subsample to reduce majority-class dominance.
XGBoost uses scale_pos_weight = negative_train_count / positive_train_count when xgboost is installed.
Gradient Boosting provides a non-linear baseline without external dependencies.
All models are evaluated on the same stratified 80/20 split.
